examples.title: Tarjan's Algorithm for Articulation Points
examples.description: Recursive algorithm to find all articulation points using DFS with discovery time and low value computation.
examples.code:
BEGIN
  GLOBAL time = 0
  GLOBAL AP[] = empty set
  
  FUNCTION FindAP(G(V, E)):
    FOR each vertex u in V:
      IF disc[u] is not set THEN
        DFS(u, parent = NIL)
    RETURN AP
  
  FUNCTION DFS(u, parent):
    disc[u] = low[u] = ++time
    children = 0
    
    FOR each neighbor v of u:
      IF disc[v] is not set THEN
        children = children + 1
        PUSH edge (u, v) onto stack
        DFS(v, u)
        
        // Update low value
        low[u] = MIN(low[u], low[v])
        
        // Check articulation point condition (non-root)
        IF parent != NIL AND low[v] >= disc[u] THEN
          ADD u to AP
        ENDIF
        
        // Extract biconnected component
        IF low[v] >= disc[u] THEN
          POP edges from stack until (u,v) removed
          PRINT popped edges as one BCC
        ENDIF
        
      ELSE IF v != parent AND disc[v] < disc[u] THEN
        // Back edge (avoid double counting)
        PUSH edge (u, v) onto stack
        low[u] = MIN(low[u], disc[v])
      ENDIF
    ENDFOR
    
    // Root articulation point condition
    IF parent == NIL AND children > 1 THEN
      ADD u to AP
    ENDIF
  END FUNCTION
END
examples.explanation: This algorithm performs a single DFS traversal. It maintains discovery times (disc) and low values. For non-root vertices, it checks if low[child] >= disc[parent] to identify articulation points. The edge stack is used to extract biconnected components when the condition is satisfied.

examples.title: Bridge Finding Algorithm
examples.description: Algorithm to find all bridges in an undirected graph using the low value concept.
examples.code:
BEGIN
  GLOBAL time = 0
  GLOBAL bridges = empty list
  
  FUNCTION FindBridges(G(V, E)):
    FOR each vertex u in V:
      disc[u] = NIL
    
    FOR each vertex u in V:
      IF disc[u] is NIL THEN
        BridgeDFS(u, parent = -1)
    
    RETURN bridges
  
  FUNCTION BridgeDFS(u, parent):
    disc[u] = low[u] = ++time
    
    FOR each neighbor v of u:
      IF disc[v] is NIL THEN
        BridgeDFS(v, u)
        low[u] = MIN(low[u], low[v])
        
        // Edge (u,v) is a bridge
        IF low[v] > disc[u] THEN
          ADD edge (u,v) to bridges
        ENDIF
        
      ELSE IF v != parent THEN
        // Back edge
        low[u] = MIN(low[u], disc[v])
      ENDIF
    ENDFOR
  END FUNCTION
END
examples.explanation: This is a simplified version focusing only on bridges. An edge (u,v) is a bridge if and only if low[v] > disc[u], meaning the subtree rooted at v cannot reach any ancestor of u without using the edge (u,v). This is stricter than the articulation point condition.

examples.title: Complete Biconnected Component Extraction
examples.description: Full algorithm with explicit edge stack management to extract all biconnected components.
examples.code:
BEGIN
  GLOBAL time = 0
  GLOBAL stack = empty stack
  GLOBAL BCCs = empty list of lists
  
  FUNCTION FindAllBCCs(G(V, E)):
    initialize disc[1..|V|] = 0, low[1..|V|] = 0
    
    FOR each vertex u in V:
      IF disc[u] == 0 THEN
        BCC-DFS(u, parent = NIL)
        
        // If any edges left in stack, they're a BCC
        IF stack is not empty THEN
          POP all edges as final BCC
        ENDIF
      ENDIF
    ENDFOR
    
    RETURN BCCs
  
  FUNCTION BCC-DFS(u, parent):
    disc[u] = low[u] = ++time
    childCount = 0
    
    FOR each neighbor v of u:
      IF disc[v] == 0 THEN
        childCount = childCount + 1
        PUSH (u, v) onto stack
        BCC-DFS(v, u)
        
        // Update low[u]
        low[u] = MIN(low[u], low[v])
        
        // Check for BCC boundary
        IF low[v] >= disc[u] THEN
          currentBCC = empty list
          REPEAT
            edge = POP from stack
            ADD edge to currentBCC
          UNTIL edge == (u, v)
          ADD currentBCC to BCCs
        ENDIF
        
      ELSE IF v != parent AND disc[v] < disc[u] THEN
        // Back edge to ancestor
        PUSH (u, v) onto stack
        low[u] = MIN(low[u], disc[v])
      ENDIF
    ENDFOR
    
    // Root is AP if it has multiple children
    IF parent == NIL AND childCount > 1 THEN
      Mark u as articulation point
    ENDIF
  END FUNCTION
END
examples.explanation: This complete algorithm handles all cases: root articulation point detection, back edge processing, and explicit edge stack management for BCC extraction. When low[child] >= disc[parent], we pop edges until the connecting edge, forming one biconnected component.
